content: read reviews/133.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/133.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/133.md.

1→# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/133.lean`
     2→
     3→Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
     4→without an explicit justification.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## 1. Copyright Header
     9→
    10→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
    11→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
    12→
    13→> Lines 1–15: exact match of the required Apache 2.0 block; no leading blank line; year is `2026`.
    14→
    15→## 2. Import Statement
    16→
    17→- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line).
    18→- [x] No other imports are present.
    19→
    20→> Line 17: `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` — sole import, one blank line after header.
    21→
    22→## 3. Module Docstring
    23→
    24→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    25→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 133`.
    26→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source.
    27→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
    28→- [x] Optional: bibliography entries for related papers.
    29→
    30→> Lines 19–35: docstring present, heading correct, reference link to `erdosproblems.com/133`, plain-English description of $f(n)$ and the question, bibliography entries for [ErPa90], [HaSe84], [FuSe94], and Alon's conjecture.
    31→
    32→## 4. Namespace
    33→
    34→- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace (`namespace Erdos133 ... end Erdos133`).
    35→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos133`).
    36→- [x] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block when opening Mathlib namespaces.
    37→
    38→> Lines 37, 39: `open SimpleGraph Filter` and `open scoped Topology` appear before `namespace Erdos133` (line 41). Namespace closed at line 134.
    39→
    40→## 5. Custom Definitions
    41→
    42→- [x] Helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations have a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    43→- [x] Definitions that involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`.
    44→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (only in theorem proof bodies).
    45→- [x] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase`.
    46→- [x] Functions returning values use appropriate naming convention.
    47→- [x] No `local notation` needed; none present.
    48→- [x] Consider adding `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks — N/A (optional).
    49→
    50→> Three custom definitions:
    51→> - `HasTriangle` (line 44): Prop, UpperCamelCase, docstring present. ✓
    52→> - `HasDiameterAtMostTwo` (line 49): Prop, UpperCamelCase, docstring present. ✓
    53→> - `erdos133_f` (line 56): `noncomputable def`, returns `ℕ`, docstring present, no `sorry`. ✓
    54→
    55→## 6. Theorem Statements
    56→
    57→- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword.
    58→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_133` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
    59→- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
    60→- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**.
    61→- [x] Main theorems and non-obvious variants have a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    62→- [x] Theorem docstrings reference source papers.
    63→
    64→> Main theorem `erdos_133` at line 68. All five variants have docstrings referencing [ErPa90], [HaSe84], [FuSe94]. All attribute lines combine `category` and `AMS` on one line.
    65→
    66→### `answer()` usage
    67→
    68→The original problem asks "Does $f(n)/\sqrt{n} \to \infty$?" — this is a **yes/no question** → **Case 1**.
    69→
    70→- [x] The theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (or solved variant thereof).
    71→- [x] Since the problem has been solved negatively, `answer(False)` is used: `answer(False) ↔ Tendsto ... atTop atTop`.
    72→- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked ($f(n)/\sqrt{n} \to \infty$), so `answer(False)` correctly means "no".
    73→- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(...)`, not as theorem parameters.
    74→
    75→> Line 68–70: `answer(False) ↔ Tendsto (fun n : ℕ => (erdos133_f n : ℝ) / Real.sqrt n) atTop atTop`. Correct: `False ↔ (f(n)/√n → ∞)` encodes "No, f(n)/√n does not tend to infinity."
    76→
    77→## 7. Category Attribute
    78→
    79→- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
    80→- [x] Values are valid:
    81→  - `research solved` on `erdos_133`, `lower_bound`, `hanson_seyffarth`, `furedi_seress`, `ratio_bounded`.
    82→  - `research open` on `alon_conjecture`.
    83→- [x] The `research open` theorem (`alon_conjecture`, line 130) contains `sorry` in its proof body.
    84→
    85→## 8. AMS Attribute
    86→
    87→- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
    88→- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed.
    89→- [x] Numbers are in ascending numeric order (single code `5`, so trivially satisfied).
    90→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    91→- [x] `5` (Combinatorics) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
    92→
    93→> All six theorems use `AMS 5`.
    94→
    95→## 9. Proof Bodies
    96→
    97→- [x] Open conjectures use `by sorry` (multi-line format with `by` at end of statement line and `sorry` indented on the next line — acceptable).
    98→- [x] No `sorry` appears in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    99→- [x] N/A: File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
   100→- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems. (The `lower_bound` variant has a proof sketch, but it is `research solved`, not `research open`. The `alon_conjecture` docstring provides only motivation/context, not a proof sketch.)
   101→- [x] No `native_decide`.
   102→
   103→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
   104→
   105→- [x] Variants are named `erdos_133.variants.<descriptor>`.
   106→- [x] All variants live in the **same file** as the main theorem.
   107→- [x] Each variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
   108→- [x] Variants that strengthen/weaken the main conjecture are documented with a brief docstring.
   109→
   110→> Five variants, all in this file:
   111→> 1. `erdos_133.variants.lower_bound` (line 82) — Moore bound lower bound.
   112→> 2. `erdos_133.variants.hanson_seyffarth` (line 97) — Hanson–Seyffarth upper bound.
   113→> 3. `erdos_133.variants.furedi_seress` (line 107) — Füredi–Seress improvement.
   114→> 4. `erdos_133.variants.ratio_bounded` (line 117) — $O(\sqrt{n})$ upper bound.
   115→> 5. `erdos_133.variants.alon_conjecture` (line 130) — open conjecture on precise constant.
   116→
   117→## 11. Code Quality
   118→
   119→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
   120→- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`∀`, `∃`, `∧`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `≤`, `≠`, `↔`, `→`).
   121→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
   122→- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
   123→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (longest ≈ 82 characters).
   124→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
   125→
   126→## 12. Build Verification
   127→
   128→- [ ] `lake build` completes with **zero errors** — **not verified** (requires build environment).
   129→- [ ] All five custom linters pass — **not verified** (requires build environment).
   130→
   131→---
   132→
   133→## Summary
   134→
   135→**Result: PASS** (pending build verification)
   136→
   137→The file is well-structured and fully conformant with all style and content requirements. The formalization clearly and correctly encodes Erdős Problem 133 as a disproved yes/no question using `answer(False) ↔ P`. The five variants cover the key known results (Moore lower bound, Hanson–Seyffarth and Füredi–Seress upper bounds, $O(\sqrt{n})$ boundedness) plus the open Alon conjecture, each with appropriate `category` classifications. All definitions have docstrings and proper naming. No issues found.
   138→

file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/133.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

> Lines 1–15: exact match of the required Apache 2.0 block; no leading blank line; year is `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line).
- [x] No other imports are present.

> Line 17: `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` — sole import, one blank line after header.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 133`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
- [x] Optional: bibliography entries for related papers.

> Lines 19–35: docstring present, heading correct, reference link to `erdosproblems.com/133`, plain-English description of $f(n)$ and the question, bibliography entries for [ErPa90], [HaSe84], [FuSe94], and Alon's conjecture.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace (`namespace Erdos133 ... end Erdos133`).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos133`).
- [x] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block when opening Mathlib namespaces.

> Lines 37, 39: `open SimpleGraph Filter` and `open scoped Topology` appear before `namespace Erdos133` (line 41). Namespace closed at line 134.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] Helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations have a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Definitions that involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (only in theorem proof bodies).
- [x] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase`.
- [x] Functions returning values use appropriate naming convention.
- [x] No `local notation` needed; none present.
- [x] Consider adding `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks — N/A (optional).

> Three custom definitions:
> - `HasTriangle` (line 44): Prop, UpperCamelCase, docstring present. ✓
> - `HasDiameterAtMostTwo` (line 49): Prop, UpperCamelCase, docstring present. ✓
> - `erdos133_f` (line 56): `noncomputable def`, returns `ℕ`, docstring present, no `sorry`. ✓

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_133` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**.
- [x] Main theorems and non-obvious variants have a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Theorem docstrings reference source papers.

> Main theorem `erdos_133` at line 68. All five variants have docstrings referencing [ErPa90], [HaSe84], [FuSe94]. All attribute lines combine `category` and `AMS` on one line.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks "Does $f(n)/\sqrt{n} \to \infty$?" — this is a **yes/no question** → **Case 1**.

- [x] The theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (or solved variant thereof).
- [x] Since the problem has been solved negatively, `answer(False)` is used: `answer(False) ↔ Tendsto ... atTop atTop`.
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked ($f(n)/\sqrt{n} \to \infty$), so `answer(False)` correctly means "no".
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(...)`, not as theorem parameters.

> Line 68–70: `answer(False) ↔ Tendsto (fun n : ℕ => (erdos133_f n : ℝ) / Real.sqrt n) atTop atTop`. Correct: `False ↔ (f(n)/√n → ∞)` encodes "No, f(n)/√n does not tend to infinity."

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Values are valid:
  - `research solved` on `erdos_133`, `lower_bound`, `hanson_seyffarth`, `furedi_seress`, `ratio_bounded`.
  - `research open` on `alon_conjecture`.
- [x] The `research open` theorem (`alon_conjecture`, line 130) contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed.
- [x] Numbers are in ascending numeric order (single code `5`, so trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` (Combinatorics) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

> All six theorems use `AMS 5`.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjectures use `by sorry` (multi-line format with `by` at end of statement line and `sorry` indented on the next line — acceptable).
- [x] No `sorry` appears in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] N/A: File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems. (The `lower_bound` variant has a proof sketch, but it is `research solved`, not `research open`. The `alon_conjecture` docstring provides only motivation/context, not a proof sketch.)
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variants are named `erdos_133.variants.<descriptor>`.
- [x] All variants live in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Each variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
- [x] Variants that strengthen/weaken the main conjecture are documented with a brief docstring.

> Five variants, all in this file:
> 1. `erdos_133.variants.lower_bound` (line 82) — Moore bound lower bound.
> 2. `erdos_133.variants.hanson_seyffarth` (line 97) — Hanson–Seyffarth upper bound.
> 3. `erdos_133.variants.furedi_seress` (line 107) — Füredi–Seress improvement.
> 4. `erdos_133.variants.ratio_bounded` (line 117) — $O(\sqrt{n})$ upper bound.
> 5. `erdos_133.variants.alon_conjecture` (line 130) — open conjecture on precise constant.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`∀`, `∃`, `∧`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `≤`, `≠`, `↔`, `→`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (longest ≈ 82 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` completes with **zero errors** — **not verified** (requires build environment).
- [ ] All five custom linters pass — **not verified** (requires build environment).

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (pending build verification)

The file is well-structured and fully conformant with all style and content requirements. The formalization clearly and correctly encodes Erdős Problem 133 as a disproved yes/no question using `answer(False) ↔ P`. The five variants cover the key known results (Moore lower bound, Hanson–Seyffarth and Füredi–Seress upper bounds, $O(\sqrt{n})$ boundedness) plus the open Alon conjecture, each with appropriate `category` classifications. All definitions have docstrings and proper naming. No issues found.

content.text: The review passed — no changes needed.
